normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_none_implies_not_mem {arr : Array Int} (target : Int) (hsorted : ∀ i j : Fin arr.size, i ≤ j → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) :
  binary_search arr target = none → (∀ i : Fin arr.size, arr[i] ≠ target)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Failure: if the algorithm returns None, then the target does not occur anywhere in the array.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_terminates {arr : Array Int} (target : Int) :
  ∃ n : Nat, binary_search_steps arr target n = true
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Termination: the binary search loop terminates (there exists a finite step bound).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_allows_any_matching_index {arr : Array Int} (target : Int) (hsorted : ∀ i j : Fin arr.size, i ≤ j → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) :
  (∃ i : Fin arr.size, arr[i] = target) →
  ∃ k : Fin arr.size, binary_search arr target = some k ∧ arr[k] = target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Duplicates are allowed: if the target occurs, the algorithm may return any matching index (not necessarily the first/last).
